notes: Anonymous horizontal linear dimension: 80mm span, _ArchTick terminators + DEFPOINTS defpoints + measurement MTEXT on DIM; a dimension cache, contains no wall geometry.

rationale.evidence: Block *D335 carries the exact AutoCAD anonymous-dimension signature: 3 POINTs on DEFPOINTS (non-plotting dimension defpoints), 2 INSERTs of the _ArchTick arrowhead/tick symbol, and one MTEXT '\A1;80' whose value equals the geometric horizontal span (80mm) between the extension-line handles (block members 8CCC..8CD6). The parallel extension LINEs are vertical and the dimension LINE is horizontal; because the span is narrow the dimension LINE is broken into short segments with the terminators flipped outboard of the witness lines (entity_count 11, LINE=5), the standard AutoCAD rendering used when the arrows and text will not fit between the extension lines. Every member sits on the annotation layer DIM.
rationale.rule: A '*D' anonymous block whose members are DEFPOINTS defpoints + dimension-terminator INSERTs (DIMDOT/_ArchTick) + a '\A1;' measurement MTEXT is the cached graphics of a dimension entity, so it is a dimension cache and its extension/dimension LINEs are annotation, never physical wall edges.
rationale.counterfactual: Had these LINEs sat on an architectural wall layer (e.g. A-WALL) as offset parallel pairs WITHOUT the DEFPOINTS defpoints, _ArchTick terminators and '\A1;' measurement MTEXT, the block would read as plan/wall geometry and both role and wall_likelihood would flip upward; the DIM layer plus the measurement MTEXT here rules that out.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection cannot reveal WHICH physical edge this dimension measures -- whether the 80mm spans a wall thickness, an opening, or an axis gap -- because an anonymous dimension block caches only its own graphics, not a back-reference to the host object it annotates.

The two parallel extension LINEs are horizontal and the dimension LINE is vertical, the canonical 2-witness + 1-dimension-line arrangement (entity_count 9, LINE=3), all on the annotation layer DIM.
rationale.rule: A '*D' anonymous block whose members are DEFPOINTS defpoints + dimension-terminator INSERTs (DIMDOT/_ArchTick) + a '\A1;' measurement MTEXT is the cached graphics of a dimension entity, so it is a dimension cache and its extension/dimension LINEs are annotation, never physical wall edges.
rationale.counterfactual: Had these LINEs sat on an architectural wall layer (e.g. A-WALL) as offset parallel pairs WITHOUT the DEFPOINTS defpoints, DIMDOT terminators and '\A1;' measurement MTEXT, the block would read as plan/wall geometry and both role and wall_likelihood would flip upward; the DIM layer plus the measurement MTEXT here rules that out.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection cannot reveal WHICH physical edge this dimension measures -- whether the 3905mm spans a wall thickness, an opening, or an axis gap -- because an anonymous dimension block caches only its own graphics, not a back-reference to the host object it annotates.
